# elasticstack_elasticsearch_security_api_key (Ephemeral Resource)

Creates an Elasticsearch API key during each Terraform plan and apply without persisting credentials to state.

See the [security API create API key documentation](https://www.elastic.co/guide/en/elasticsearch/reference/current/security-api-create-api-key.html) and [create cross-cluster API key documentation](https://www.elastic.co/guide/en/elasticsearch/reference/current/security-api-create-cross-cluster-api-key.html) for more details.

Use the managed [`elasticstack_elasticsearch_security_api_key`](/docs/resources/elasticsearch_security_api_key) resource when credentials should remain in Terraform state.

~> **Warning:** Do not combine `invalidate_on_close = true` with a persistent secret store (for example HashiCorp Vault or AWS SSM). The key is invalidated when the Terraform run completes, so any credential copied into external storage becomes unusable immediately afterward.

~> **Warning:** Each `terraform plan` and `terraform apply` creates a new API key. When `invalidate_on_close = false`, strongly recommend setting `expiration` to prevent unlimited key accumulation in Elasticsearch.

-> **Note:** `Open()` runs during both `terraform plan` and `terraform apply`, not only during apply. Every plan creates a new API key.

-> **Note:** If Terraform is interrupted before `Close()` runs, an API key created with `invalidate_on_close = true` may remain valid until it expires naturally (or indefinitely when no `expiration` is set).

## Example Usage

### Persistent pattern (store in Vault, never in `.tfstate`)

```terraform
ephemeral "elasticstack_elasticsearch_security_api_key" "vault_key" {
  name       = "app-key"
  expiration = "30d"

  role_descriptors = jsonencode({
    my-role = {
      cluster = ["monitor"]
      indices = [{
        names      = ["logs-*"]
        privileges = ["read"]
      }]
    }
  })

  # invalidate_on_close defaults to false
}

resource "vault_kv_secret_v2" "creds" {
  mount = "secret"
  name  = "elastic/api-key"

  data_json = jsonencode({
    encoded = ephemeral.elasticstack_elasticsearch_security_api_key.vault_key.encoded
  })
}
```

### In-run pattern (key valid only during this apply)

```terraform
ephemeral "elasticstack_elasticsearch_security_api_key" "inrun_key" {
  name                = "seed-job-key"
  invalidate_on_close = true

  role_descriptors = jsonencode({
    my-role = {
      cluster = ["monitor"]
      indices = [{
        names      = ["logs-*"]
        privileges = ["read"]
      }]
    }
  })
}

resource "null_resource" "seed" {
  triggers = {
    always = timestamp()
  }

  provisioner "local-exec" {
    command = "./seed.sh"
    environment = {
      ES_API_KEY = ephemeral.elasticstack_elasticsearch_security_api_key.inrun_key.encoded
    }
  }
}
```

## Schema

### Required

- `name` (String) Specifies the name for this API key.

### Optional

- `access` (Attributes) Access configuration for cross-cluster API keys. Only applicable when type is `cross_cluster`.
- `elasticsearch_connection` (Block List) Elasticsearch connection configuration block.
- `expiration` (String) Expiration time for the API key. By default, API keys never expire. Strongly recommended when `invalidate_on_close` is false.
- `invalidate_on_close` (Boolean) When true, invalidates the API key after the Terraform run completes. Defaults to false.
- `metadata` (String) Arbitrary metadata that you want to associate with the API key.
- `role_descriptors` (String) Role descriptors for this API key. Valid only when `type` is `rest`.
- `type` (String) The type of API key. Valid values are `rest` (default) and `cross_cluster`.

### Result (computed during Open; never written to state)

- `api_key` (String, Sensitive) Generated API key secret.
- `encoded` (String, Sensitive) Base64-encoded `id:api_key` credentials.
- `expiration_timestamp` (Number) Expiration time in milliseconds for the API key. `0` when the key does not expire.
- `key_id` (String) Unique id for this API key.
